scenarios.opening_query: The 'certified pre-owned' SUV we bought has a salvage title we discovered at registration.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Criminal Law > White Collar Crime
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DMV flagged the title as washed through two states and referred the dealer to the DOJ's title-fraud task force — we're witnesses in a criminal case now, and I need to understand restitution.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DMV flagged a washed title and referred the dealer to the fraud task force — we're witnesses now, and I need to understand restitution.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a criminal title-fraud case encompasses the loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No referral — the dealer claims an 'auction paperwork error' and it's our civil fraud case to bring.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No referral — the dealer blames 'auction paperwork,' so it's our civil case.

scenarios.opening_query: I paid for a full mechanical inspection before buying this car and it turned out to be worthless paper.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: My claim is against the inspection shop, not the seller — they certified a cracked head and shot transmission as 'excellent,' and the private seller genuinely didn't know.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the inspection shop — they certified a cracked head and dead transmission as 'excellent.' The private seller didn't know.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the inspection service, not the seller, is the defendant
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The shop's inspection listed the problems — the dealer showed us a doctored copy with those lines removed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The inspection listed the problems — the dealer showed us a doctored copy without them.

scenarios.opening_query: Our RV dealer sold us a rig too heavy for its own axles and now refuses to acknowledge the danger.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The axle failed on the interstate — we crashed, my wife's vertebra is fractured, and the manufacturer's weight ratings were falsified per the recall investigation.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The axle failed on the interstate — my wife fractured a vertebra, and the recall investigation says the maker falsified the ratings.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the defect caused a crash injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No failure yet — we caught it at a weigh station; we want the sale rescinded before someone gets hurt.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No failure yet — a weigh station caught it. We want the sale undone before someone's hurt.

scenarios.opening_query: I traded in my car and the dealership never paid off its loan like the contract said they would.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Banking || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The dealership closed and its floor-plan bank now claims MY trade-in loan follows me — my credit shows 60-day lates on a car I don't own; the lender fight is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The dealer closed and their bank says my old loan follows me — 60-day lates on a car I don't even own. The lender's the fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the surviving lender's conduct is the operative dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The dealer's still open and solvent — they just 'haven't gotten to it,' and the dealer-practices case writes itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The dealer's open and solvent — they just 'haven't gotten to it.'
scenarios.vetting_note: Dealer-collapse fact shifts the defendant to the bank.

scenarios.opening_query: The classic car I restored and sold is now the subject of angry letters from the buyer's lawyer.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I've restored and sold nine cars in three years with a resale license — the lawyer argues I'm a dealer in the law's eyes, which changes my duties and exposure entirely.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I've sold nine restorations in three years under a resale license — his lawyer says that makes me a dealer legally, which changes everything.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller's volume makes them a merchant with dealer duties
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: This was my one and only restoration — a genuine hobby sale between individuals.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was my one and only — a genuine hobby sale.
scenarios.vetting_note: Merchant-status by volume; the seller's own history is the routing fact.

scenarios.opening_query: A mobile mechanic 'fixed' my brakes in my driveway and I crashed into my own garage the next morning.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: The crash broke my ankle and wrist — the injury claim against him leads, with the garage damage attached.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I broke my ankle and wrist in the crash — the injury claim leads, with the garage damage attached.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I wasn't hurt — it's the redone brake job, the garage door, and my bumper: money damages for bad service.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I wasn't hurt — it's the brake redo, garage door, and bumper. Bad-service damages.
